evals.prompt: I want to add a /health endpoint to our FastAPI service that returns 200 OK with `{"status": "ok"}`. The service is in src/api/main.py. We use pytest. Can you implement this end-to-end with tests first?
evals.expected_output: Agent follows dev-workflow: explores existing routes, writes a failing pytest test that hits /health, adds the route, confirms test passes, produces a tidy commit-ready diff with no scope creep. Should_trigger: true.

evals.prompt: I'm starting a brand-new Python CLI tool from scratch. Empty directory. I want it set up for use with Claude Code and Cursor with AGENTS.md, a pyproject.toml using uv, and an ISSUES.md tracker. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes this is project-init territory, NOT dev-workflow. dev-workflow should not trigger for empty-directory / scaffold-from-scratch requests. Should_trigger: false — project-init is the correct skill.

evals.prompt: Our test_auth_token_refresh test started failing on CI this morning after someone merged a refactor to the JWT module. It passes locally for me but fails on CI with a cryptic 'invalid signature' error. Can you help me figure out what's going on and fix it?
evals.expected_output: Agent follows dev-workflow Phase 2-B (Bug Fix): reproduces the failure, identifies root cause via the commit history, writes a regression test, fixes minimally, verifies. Should_trigger: true.
